title: Gravity Flip Stack
one_liner: Stack blocks by flipping gravity — the floor becomes the ceiling every time you tap.
elevator_pitch: Most stacking games are passive and predictable. Gravity Flip Stack inverts the core loop: tap to flip gravity, and blocks fall from whichever direction is currently 'down'. Players must read the rhythm of the flip to build a tower upward and downward simultaneously, creating a symmetric skyscraper challenge that gets chaotic fast.
core_mechanic: Gravity inversion on tap to stack blocks from both directions simultaneously
infinite_stage_design: Tower height target increases each stage; block widths shrink and fall speed increases; at stage 5+ random gravity auto-flips appear; stage 10+ introduces rotating blocks.
difficulty_curve: Early stages: wide blocks, slow fall, generous timing. Mid stages: blocks narrow, speed increases, timing windows shrink. Late stages: random auto-flips, double blocks, speed bursts.
visual_style: Clean minimal SVG — flat geometric blocks in neon color pairs (coral/teal), particle burst on perfect stack, tower silhouette visible at all times.
sound_concept: Satisfying 'thunk' on stack, pitch-ascending combo chain, low gravity-whoosh on flip, upbeat lo-fi background loop.
monetization_hooks: skin packs for block shapes and colors
viral_hooks: screenshot-worthy symmetric tower at session end
market_reasoning: Stacking games (like Stack by Ketchapp) dominated charts in 2015-2018 and still perform on casual charts, but no major title has combined stacking with gravity inversion. Gravity-flip as a mechanic is proven viral (Flappy Bird, Swing Copters), yet the stacking genre has not adopted it. This is a direct blue ocean: familiar stacking ritual + surprise gravity mechanic = instant comprehension, high replayability.
creator: trendsetter

title: Crowd Rush Merge
one_liner: Merge your crowd as you run — absorb people, combine numbers, crush the gate.
elevator_pitch: Crowd runner games (Count Masters, Crowd City) are top 10 fixtures, and merge games are a perennial top-grossing genre — but nobody has fused them into a single real-time action loop. Crowd Rush Merge lets players swipe to steer a numbered crowd, merge same-numbered runners on the fly, and smash through gates that require a specific crowd size, all in a never-ending runner that escalates in gate complexity and lane width.
core_mechanic: Steer a numbered crowd, merge matching runners mid-run to hit gate thresholds
infinite_stage_design: Each stage adds more gate types (multiply, subtract, divide), narrows lanes to force precision merges, introduces enemy crowds that shrink your number, and boss gates with very high thresholds requiring perfect merge chains.
difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: add gates only, wide lanes. Stage 4-7: multiply/divide gates appear, lanes tighten. Stage 8+: enemy runners split your crowd, boss gates require 500+ merged runners.
visual_style: Top-down minimal runner — round SVG people icons color-coded by number tier, bold gate arches with arithmetic symbols, confetti burst on successful gate pass.
sound_concept: Satisfying crowd 'pop' on merge, escalating crowd cheer as number grows, gate smash boom, upbeat arcade loop.
monetization_hooks: gate skip power-ups for tough divide gates
viral_hooks: end-screen showing 'you merged X people' total stat
challenge mode: hit exactly the gate number — no more, no less
market_reasoning: Crowd runner is a proven top-chart hyper-casual genre (Count Masters has 100M+ downloads). Merge mechanics are the #1 retained casual genre in 2024-2025. The two have never been fused in real-time — all merge games are turn-based puzzle boards. Real-time merge-while-running is a mechanic gap no major title occupies, and the combination has natural viral shareability from satisfying number explosions.
creator: trendsetter
